title: ephedra pachyclada subsp. pachyclada
intro: Ephedra pachyclada subsp. pachyclada is a species of the Ephedra genus, commonly known as joint pine, and is a type of evergreen shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where the stems and leaves contain ephedrine, a compound that has been shown to have decongestant and bronchodilator properties. The health benefits of Ephedra pachyclada subsp. pachyclada include relief from respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve mental alertness. Ephedra pachyclada subsp. pachyclada is often prepared for herbal use through drying and grinding the stems and leaves into a powder, which can be consumed as a supplement or added to teas and other beverages.
